talk_title: Developing CAR T‑cell therapies for T‑cell malignancies

speaker.name: Dr. John F. DiPersio
speaker.affiliation: Washington University School of Medicine, St. Louis, Director of the Section of Cellular Therapy and the Center for Gene and Cellular Immunotherapy

biomarkers.name: CD7 loss
biomarkers.finding: Observed in ~20% of relapsed patients after UCART7 therapy, often due to biallelic deletion or splicing abnormalities

biomarkers.name: CD2 loss
biomarkers.finding: Most relapsed cases after CD2‑CAR therapy showed loss of CD2 surface expression

biomarkers.name: CR rate
biomarkers.finding: 82% complete remission at the recommended Phase II dose in the first‑in‑human UCART7 study (n not specified)

models.name: UCART7 Phase I study (Blood 2025)
models.genotype_or_key_features: Patients with relapsed/refractory T‑cell ALL, CD7‑high disease

models.name: T‑cell lymphoma xenograft model
models.genotype_or_key_features: CD2‑positive lymphoma cell lines in immunodeficient mice

clinical_data.population: relapsed/refractory T‑cell ALL, Phase I UCART7 cohort

MOA_summary: The work introduces universal allogeneic CAR‑T platforms that multiplex‑edit donor T cells to delete CD7 (and TCRα) for T‑ALL and to delete CD2 (and TCRα) for T‑cell lymphomas, enabling potent antigen‑specific killing while avoiding fratricide. Epitope‑masking of CD2 or incorporation of the CD2 cytoplasmic tail restores costimulatory signaling lost after CD2 knockout. Long‑acting IL‑7 fusion (NTi‑7) administered early after infusion dramatically expands CAR‑T persistence and cures murine models. Small‑molecule PI3γδ and JAK inhibitors, or CD40L blockade, suppress IL‑6–driven CRS without impairing cytotoxicity.
key_findings: Multiplex CRISPR editing of donor T cells to delete CD7 and TCRα enables universal CAR‑T cells that achieved an 82% complete remission rate in the first‑in‑human UCART7 Phase I trial for relapsed T‑ALL.

Deletion of CD2 in CAR‑T cells caused severe functional exhaustion in vitro and loss of antitumor activity in vivo, which was rescued by epitope‑masking of CD2 or by inserting the CD2 cytoplasmic domain into the CAR construct.

Long‑acting IL‑7 (NTi‑7) given between days 1‑7 after CAR‑T infusion expands CAR‑T cells 10,000‑ to 100,000‑fold and cures murine models of T‑cell lymphoma, B‑cell lymphoma, and AML, whereas later dosing is ineffective.

A high‑throughput screen of 700 kinase inhibitors identified PI3γδ inhibitor (Devolasib) and JAK1/2 inhibitor (Ruxolitinib) as synergistic suppressors of IL‑6 production without impairing CAR‑T cytotoxicity; combined prophylaxis eliminated grade ≥2 CRS in a randomized patient cohort.

CD4⁺ CAR‑T cells are the primary source of IL‑6 via membrane‑bound CD40L, while CD8⁺ cells mediate killing; CD40L knockout or blockade markedly reduces CRS, highlighting a mechanistic link between CD40L signaling and cytokine release.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Relapse after UCART7 is frequently associated with loss of CD7 surface expression through biallelic deletion or splicing alterations.
CD2‑CAR‑T cells lose efficacy when CD2 is knocked out because CD2 provides essential costimulatory signaling; epitope‑masked CD2 restores function.
Targeting CD7 and CD2 depletes normal NK and T cells, leading to profound immunodeficiency and infection risk.

combination_strategies.rationale: Early cytokine support enhances CAR‑T expansion, persistence, and antitumor efficacy
combination_strategies.phenotype: 10,000‑100,000‑fold CAR‑T expansion; complete tumor eradication in murine models

combination_strategies.rationale: Dual inhibition suppresses IL‑6–driven CRS without compromising CAR‑T cytotoxicity
combination_strategies.phenotype: Near‑complete abrogation of grade ≥2 CRS in randomized patient cohort; maintained tumor killing

open_questions: How can the profound immunodeficiency caused by CD7/CD2 depletion be mitigated while preserving antitumor activity?
What are the molecular mechanisms underlying CD7 downregulation in relapsed patients (e.g., splice variants vs. genomic loss)?
Can the optimal timing and dosing of IL‑7 be refined for different CAR‑T products and disease settings?
